The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it; set `side` to 'right' (default) | 'left' | 'top' | 'bottom'. Put your drawer body markup directly in the data-lv-drawer-body region of the copied template (owned source, NOT a slot). Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-drawer-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the server `close` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=close button), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). For the richer header/description/footer four-side panel use the sheet wire instead. Adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: dropdown-menu
items.description: A trigger + a menu of REAL items (links / actions), with ZERO JS. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 2/3) built on the popover overlay seam: show/hide via the native HTML `popover` attribute + `popovertarget` (top-layer, light-dismiss, aria-expanded wired by the browser); position via CSS Anchor Positioning; items are real <a href> (navigate JS-off, middle-click works) or real <button> (form action), each role=menuitem (WAI-ARIA APG menu-button, Radix DropdownMenu model). Replaces the <lv-dropdown-menu> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean. Tab navigates the items (the platform); arrow-key roving is an optional typed-TS enhancer, not shipped.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu partial -- a trigger + a menu of REAL items (links / actions), with ZERO shipped JS. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 2/3): the Lit island is gone. Built on the SAME overlay seam as the popover partial (native `popover` attribute + CSS Anchor Positioning). TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL, not WIRE): a dropdown menu, once you accept the server-pure overlay, is "pure display, no client state" -- the items are server-owned DATA the controller already has, the entries are real /
${content}

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/item partial -- one REAL menu entry of a dropdown-menu (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). Renders a real when `href` is given (navigation: the browser navigates, middle-click / open-in-new-tab work), otherwise a real @endif

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/separator partial -- a non-selectable divider between dropdown-menu groups (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). Pure CSS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A token-styled hairline carrying role="separator" so assistive tech announces the group boundary. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG): role="separator" inside the role="menu" panel. Params: (none) Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.separator() --%>

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/label partial -- a non-interactive caption for a group of menu items (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). The shadcn DropdownMenuLabel: a small medium-weight heading that titles a DropdownMenuGroup; it is NOT a menuitem (no role, not focusable), so assistive tech reads it as static text within the menu, not as a selectable entry. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure token-styled text. STRUCTURE (shadcn DropdownMenuLabel, new-york-v4): px-2 py-1.5 text-sm font-medium, with an `inset` variant (data-[inset]:pl-8) that aligns the label text under the items' icon column when the group has checkable/icon items. A11y: a plain text caption (no role). Paired with dropdown-menu/group it can name the group via aria-labelledby (pass a matching `id`). Params: String label the caption text. String id = "" optional id, so a dropdown-menu/group can aria-labelledby it. boolean inset = false indents the label under the items' leading-icon column (shadcn inset). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the label. Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.label(label = "My account") --%> @param String label @param String id = "" @param boolean inset = false @param String cssClass = ""
${label}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/group partial -- a semantic grouping of related menu items inside a dropdown-menu (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). The shadcn DropdownMenuGroup: it bundles items that belong together (separated from other groups by a dropdown-menu/separator), and carries role="group" so assistive tech announces the boundary; pass a `labelId` that matches a dropdown-menu/label `id` to name the group (aria-labelledby). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. A wrapper; the items are OWNED, server-rendered. STRUCTURE (shadcn DropdownMenuGroup, Radix Group): a plain wrapper element. shadcn's div has no role; we add role="group" (WAI-ARIA APG menu grouping) so the group boundary is announced, the accessible-faithful choice for a labelled group of menuitems. A11y: role="group" inside the role="menu" panel; aria-labelledby -> a dropdown-menu/label id. Params: gg.jte.Content content the group's items (dropdown-menu/item entries), OWNED. String labelId = "" id of a dropdown-menu/label that names this group. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the group. Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.label(label = "Account", id = "acct-lbl") @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.group(labelId = "acct-lbl", content = @@` @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(href = "/profile", label = "Profile") @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item(href = "/settings", label = "Settings") `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String labelId = "" @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/shortcut partial -- the right-aligned keyboard hint of a menu item (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). The shadcn DropdownMenuShortcut: ml-auto text-xs tracking-widest text-muted-foreground -- decorative text pinned to the item's end showing the accelerator (e.g. "Cmd+S"). It is announced as decoration, not as an action. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure token-styled text. Back-compat: dropdown-menu/item still accepts a `shortcut` String and renders the same visual inline; this partial is the standalone shadcn-faithful data-slot for callers composing an item's trailing region by hand (e.g. inside a custom item body). A11y: aria-hidden="true" -- the glyph is a visual hint, the real binding lives in the platform. Params: String shortcut the accelerator text (e.g. "Cmd+S", "Del"). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the shortcut. Usage: @@template.lievit.dropdown-menu.shortcut(shortcut = "Cmd+S") --%> @param String shortcut @param String cssClass = "" ${shortcut}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dropdown-menu/sub partial -- a NESTED submenu inside a dropdown-menu (Wave 2/3, server-first, no JS). Bundles the shadcn DropdownMenuSub + DropdownMenuSubTrigger + DropdownMenuSubContent triple into one server-pure disclosure: a native
whose is the SubTrigger (role="menuitem", aria-haspopup="menu", a trailing chevron-right) and whose body is the SubContent (role="menu") holding the nested items. TIER DECISION (why this shape, server-first): a true Radix flyout submenu reveals on hover / arrow-right and floats beside the parent -- that hover/arrow reveal + repositioning is the ONE piece that genuinely needs JS. The zero-JS, CSP-clean equivalent is the native
disclosure: the SubTrigger toggles the nested menu OPEN INLINE (the browser owns the open state, no script), the items are real /

items.docs: Copies the dropdown-menu family to your JTE root (lievit/dropdown-menu.jte + lievit/dropdown-menu/{item,separator,label,group,shortcut,sub}.jte), reached as @template.lievit.dropdown-menu(...). Pass a unique `id`, the `trigger` content, and compose the `content` slot from @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.item (give `href` for a navigation link, or `formId`/`formAction` for a form action; type=checkbox/radio renders the checked-state indicator glyph), @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.label (a group caption, inset to align under the indicator column), @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.group (role=group, aria-labelledby a label id), @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.shortcut (standalone keyboard hint), @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.sub (a nested submenu via native <details>, server-first; the hover/arrow flyout reveal is a deferrable typed-TS enhancer) and @template.lievit.dropdown-menu.separator. The browser owns show/hide + dismiss; CSS anchor positioning places the menu. Real links navigate with JS off. For a menu whose ITEMS are a server feed (a filtered command palette), use the command WIRE component instead.

items.name: field
items.description: The FormField + FieldError orchestration: a label + control + description/error wrapper that auto-derives the accessible wiring (aria-invalid, the description/error ids the control points its aria-describedby at, the destructive label cue) from the per-field error, the server-first analogue of shadcn's FormControl/FormMessage. FieldError supports a single message or a deduped multi-error <ul> (the errors list), and the Field root cascades destructive text to its labels when invalid. Supports vertical/horizontal/responsive orientation and a FieldContent slot. Ships the FieldGroup, FieldSet/FieldLegend (legend/label variant), FieldTitle and FieldSeparator child partials.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui field/group partial -- shadcn's FieldGroup: the vertical stack that holds a set of @@template.lievit.field calls (and FieldSeparators), and establishes the container-query context that drives a field's "responsive" orientation. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout, no client state. It is the `@container/field-group` parent shadcn uses so an inner Field with orientation="responsive" flips from stacked to side-by-side at the `@md` breakpoint of THIS group's width (not the viewport), so a field group in a narrow column stays stacked. STRUCTURE: shadcn's FieldGroup is a `gap`-ed flex column marked `@container/field-group`; the responsive Field reads it via `@md/field-group:`. We reproduce that exactly with tokens. A11y: a plain grouping wrapper; the grouped controls carry their own labels. For a semantic group with a single legend, use @@template.lievit.field.set instead. Params: gg.jte.Content content the fields (and separators) in this group. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the group. Usage: @@template.lievit.field.group(content = @@` @@template.lievit.field(forId = "first", label = "First name", control = @@`...`) @@template.lievit.field.separator() @@template.lievit.field(forId = "last", label = "Last name", control = @@`...`) `) --%> @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = ""
${content}

items.name: file-upload
items.description: A server-rendered drag-and-drop dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path: a native <input type=file l:upload> POSTs the bytes out-of-band, the endpoint returns a signed temp-file reference, and the wire sets the server-held file list (the bytes never ride the snapshot). Remove drops a file from the server-held list. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-file-upload> Lit island. WAI-ARIA dropzone-as-button + labelled file list. The runtime upload directive (l:upload) is ready (runtime/features/uploads.ts).

files.content:
@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import io.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent @import io.lievit.wire.FileUploadComponent.UploadedFile @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui file-upload WIRE template -- a server-rendered dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/file-upload/FileUploadComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The dropzone + the selected-file list are server-rendered HTML. The upload itself is the wire runtime's upload directive: the native POSTs the chosen bytes out-of-band to the upload endpoint (runtime features/uploads.ts), which returns a signed temp-file reference; the wire sets the bound `files` field to that reference and re-renders this list. The bytes never ride the snapshot (state-never-code), only the reference does. A remove arms the file name via $set then a confirm action drops it from the server-held list. No . Runtime upload directive: READY (runtime/features/uploads.ts installs l:upload). The adopter wires the SERVER half (the /lievit/upload controller that stores to temp + returns the signed ref); that is application-specific. If you prefer a plain multipart POST instead of the wire upload, swap the l:upload input for inside a
and handle the upload in a controller (the partial recipe). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG dropzone-as-button + a labelled file list (the model the dropped island cited): the zone is role="button" tabindex=0 with aria-describedby -> the hint; the native is visually hidden but reachable; each file row has a labelled remove button. Native input + l:upload beats a custom uploader: the platform owns the file picker and drag-drop, the wire owns the transport, the server owns the list. A11y: div[role=button][aria-label][aria-describedby], visually-hidden input[type=file], ul[aria-label="Selected files"] with per-row aria-label="Remove ". Params: accept String comma-separated MIME types / extensions for the native input multiple boolean allow multiple files (locked) disabled boolean disable the control (locked) dropLabel String primary label inside the dropzone hint String constraint hint below the label label String accessible label for the dropzone button pendingRemove String the file name armed for removal (set via $set, confirmed by removePending) _instance FileUploadComponent the live instance (reads the server-held file list) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, point l:upload at your endpoint, persist refs on submit): register FileUploadComponent as a PROTOTYPE @Bean; install the wire upload runtime; mount it. --%> @param String accept @param boolean multiple @param boolean disabled @param String dropLabel @param String hint @param String label @param String pendingRemove @param FileUploadComponent _instance @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var inputId = "lv-file-input";} !{var hintId = "lv-file-hint";} !{List files = _instance.files();}
@if(!files.isEmpty())
    @for(UploadedFile file : files)
  • ${file.name()} ${FileUploadComponent.formatSize(file.size())} @if(pendingRemove.equals(file.name())) @else @endif
    • @endfor
@endif

items.docs: Register FileUploadComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). The native input carries l:upload="files": install the wire upload runtime (installUploads from runtime/features/uploads.ts) and wire the SERVER half (the /lievit/upload controller that validates + stores to temp + returns the signed ref). The dropzone + file list render server-side from the `files` field; a remove arms the file name via $set then the removePending action drops it. If you prefer a plain multipart POST, swap the l:upload input for <input type=file name=...> inside <form method=post enctype=multipart/form-data>. Adjust the template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: form
items.description: The server-first Form: a native <form> wrapper that owns a form-level validation message region (role=alert) and stacks its field children, the server-rendered analogue of shadcn's Form (FormProvider) + a top-level FormMessage for the error not tied to a single field. Pairs with the field partial, which does the per-field aria wiring. JS-off it POSTs natively; with the wire runtime the same markup submits over the wire.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui form partial -- the server-first Form: a native
wrapper that owns a form-level validation message region and stacks its @@template.lievit.field children. The server-rendered analogue of shadcn's Form (FormProvider) + a top-level FormMessage for the error that is not tied to one field (e.g. "Invalid email or password"). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. No client state: validation runs server-side, the controller passes `error` (form-level) + each field's error down, and this partial does the aria wiring. JS-off the form POSTs natively; with the wire runtime present the same markup submits over the wire. This is why shadcn's Form has no real counterpart here beyond layout-only Field: the missing piece was the server validation->message orchestration, which is this + field.jte. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's Form is react-hook-form's FormProvider (pure JS context). A server-first stack has no client form context to provide: the truth is the POST and the controller's validation result. So the "Form" here is the native + a form-level message slot. Field-level wiring (id/aria-describedby/aria-invalid) is auto-derived in @@template.lievit.field from the per-field error; the form-level error gets its own role=alert region whose id (`-error`) the form references via aria-describedby when present. A11y (WAI-ARIA): a native (the platform form landmark). The form-level error is a `role="alert" aria-live="assertive"` region with id `-error`, referenced by the form's aria-describedby only while present, so a screen reader announces the submit failure. Each field carries its own message via @@template.lievit.field. Params: String id = null the form id (derives the form-level message id `-error`). String action = null the form action URL (native POST target). String method = "post" native method. String error = null form-level validation message (not tied to a single field). gg.jte.Content content the form body (fields, field groups, actions). gg.jte.Content header = null optional header content rendered above the error region. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the . Usage: @@template.lievit.form(id = "login", action = "/login", error = errors.form(), content = @@` @@template.lievit.field(forId = "email", label = "Email", error = errors.email(), control = @@`@@template.lievit.input(name = "email", id = "email", describedBy = "email-error", invalid = errors.email() != null)`) @@template.lievit.button(type = "submit", content = @@`Sign in`) `) --%> @param String id = null @param String action = null @param String method = "post" @param String error = null @param gg.jte.Content content @param gg.jte.Content header = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var hasError = error != null && !error.isBlank();} !{var formId = (id != null && !id.isBlank()) ? id : null;} !{var errorId = formId != null ? formId + "-error" : "form-error";} @if(header != null)${header}@endif @if(hasError)
${error}
@endif ${content}

items.name: hover-card
items.description: A hover/focus-triggered preview card, CSS-only (Radix preview model: no ARIA role, aria-hidden, not focus-trapping). Anchors below the trigger with align (start/center/end) + sideOffset. Content lives in the DOM, revealed by group-hover/group-focus-within. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui hover-card partial -- a hover/focus-triggered preview card, CSS-only (no JS). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island used Floating UI + open/close timers + Lit state. Per ADR-0012 the server-pure path is CSS: the panel content is ALREADY in the DOM and is revealed by the group's :hover / :focus-within, with no client state and no positioning JS. The open/close delay nuance is dropped deliberately (a delay needs a timer = client state); the gap between trigger and panel is kept crossable by laying the panel directly under the trigger with a small token offset. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): Radix's hover-card MODEL (preview affordance, not an announced popup) rendered with a CSS group-hover reveal. Tailwind's group + group-hover / group-focus-within utilities express "show the panel when the trigger area is hovered or focused" without any script -- the canonical CSS-only disclosure. A preview card sits in normal flow under its trigger (side=bottom, the Radix default); no CSS Anchor Positioning needed. POSITIONING (server-first equivalent of Radix align/sideOffset): shadcn places HoverCardContent with align=center default + sideOffset=4. We map `align` (start/center/end) to the horizontal anchoring under the trigger and `sideOffset` to the token gap below it, computed at render time; `data-align` mirrors Radix's data attribute so a stylesheet can hook it. side stays bottom (a preview reads below its trigger); flip it in your own copy if a layout needs it. A11y (Radix preview model): the trigger is a normal interactive element; the panel carries NO ARIA role and is aria-hidden="true" -- it is a sighted-pointer/keyboard preview, so the same information MUST also be reachable in the page flow for assistive tech. Not focus-trapping. Params: gg.jte.Content trigger the trigger element (a link / button / text). gg.jte.Content content the preview body, revealed on hover/focus. String align = "center" one of: start | center | end (horizontal anchoring). String sideOffset = null the gap below the trigger (a token; defaults to the level-1 spacing token when null). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wrapper. Usage: @@template.lievit.hover-card( trigger = @@`@@ada`, content = @@`

Ada Lovelace, first programmer.

` ) @@template.lievit.hover-card(align = "end", trigger = @@`...`, content = @@`...`) --%> @param gg.jte.Content trigger @param gg.jte.Content content @param String align = "center" @param String sideOffset = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var customOffset = sideOffset != null && !sideOffset.isBlank();} <%-- The gap below the trigger. By DEFAULT the level-1 spacing token, applied as a bracketed Tailwind margin-top class (so the dimension reads a spacing token); a caller sideOffset overrides it inline. --%> !{var offsetClass = customOffset ? "" : "mt-[var(--lv-space-1)]";} !{var offsetStyle = customOffset ? "margin-top:" + sideOffset + ";" : "";} <%-- align maps to the horizontal anchoring of the preview under its trigger. --%> !{var alignPos = switch (align) { case "center" -> "left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%);"; case "end" -> "right:0;"; default -> "left:0;"; // start };} ${trigger} ${content}

items.name: icon
items.description: Inline Lucide <svg> by name, sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens. Zero JS, CSP-clean. The root PARTIAL: every partial/wire template that needs a glyph composes it via @template.lievit.icon(name=...).

files.content:
@import static io.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui icon partial -- renders an inline Lucide <svg> by name.

  DELIVERY DECISION (scientific, issue #428): inline-per-name, NOT an SVG sprite.
  Compared against MUI (font/SVG components), Radix (per-icon SVG components), shadcn
  (lucide-react, per-icon, tree-shaken) and Web Awesome (<wa-icon>, font/registry):
    (1) tree-shakeability -- inline-per-name wins: only the icons vendored into
        registry/icons/ exist in the body map, so an adopter ships exactly what they use.
        A sprite ships the whole <symbol> sheet (or needs a build to assemble a per-page
        subset) and an extra request / external <use href> with its CSP+cross-doc quirks.
    (2) ergonomics for a JTE adopter -- "add an icon" = drop a .svg in registry/icons/ and
        re-run generate-icon-map.mjs. No sheet to maintain, no <use> id registry.
    (3) robustness -- no <use href>, no external ref, CSP-clean, zero JS.
  The sprite's only edge (repeated-icon byte savings) is marginal in a gestionale and costs
  the copy-in simplicity, so inline-per-name is objectively best here.

  The uniform <svg> wrapper lives HERE once (sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens); the per-icon
  inner body (paths/circles/...) comes from io.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body(name) -- a lievit-OWNED
  facade over an IconResolver SPI, defaulting to the bundled, tree-shaken Lucide set
  (LucideIconResolver). The partial names NO adopter class, so it renders standalone out of the box.
  To ship a different icon set, implement io.lievit.ui.IconResolver and call
  LievitIcons.setResolver(...) once at startup; this call site never changes. The Lucide SVGs
  stroke with currentColor, so colour follows the surrounding text colour or any Tailwind `text-*`
  utility / --lv-color-* override on a wrapping element.

  Usage:
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "trash-2", cssClass = "text-[var(--lv-color-danger)]")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "search", size = "1.25rem")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "settings", label = "Open settings")   // labelled (img role)

  By default the icon is decorative: aria-hidden="true" + focusable="false". Pass a `label`
  to expose it to assistive tech (role="img" + aria-label); then it is NOT aria-hidden.

  Add an icon: drop registry/icons/<name>.svg (any Lucide SVG), run
  `node registry/icons/generate-icon-map.mjs`, commit the regenerated map.
--%>
@param String name
@param String size = "var(--lv-icon-size)"
@param String cssClass = ""
@param String label = null
<svg
  xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  viewBox="0 0 24 24"
  width="${size}"
  height="${size}"
  fill="none"
  stroke="currentColor"
  stroke-width="var(--lv-icon-stroke)"
  stroke-linecap="round"
  stroke-linejoin="round"
  class="lv-icon ${cssClass}"
  role="${label == null ? null : "img"}"
  aria-hidden="${label == null ? "true" : null}"
  aria-label="${label}"
  focusable="false"
  data-lucide="${name}"
>$unsafe{body(name)}</svg>

It renders standalone out of the box: the partial imports io.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body(name), a lievit-OWNED facade over an IconResolver SPI defaulting to the bundled, tree-shaken Lucide set (LucideIconResolver) -- all three Java files (IconResolver, LucideIconResolver, LievitIcons) are copied to your io/lievit/ui/ source root, so NO adopter class is needed. Add an icon: drop registry/icons/<name>.svg and re-run registry/icons/generate-icon-map.mjs (regenerates LucideIconResolver). items.name: infolist-entry
items.description:
The read-only DISPLAY leaf of a detail page: the value variants the kit's infolist entries resolve to, one partial driven by a `type` switch (color | icon | image | code | text). Mirrors io.lievit.kit.schema.infolist's read-only entries (the filament-infolists types carried over): ColorEntry (a swatch + the CSS value), IconEntry (a tinted Lucide icon + optional label), ImageEntry (a sized/circular tile), CodeEntry (a monospace 
 block with language + line-number affordances). Each branch reuses an existing primitive (icon / badge / avatar-style tile) rather than re-implementing. The READ counterpart of a form control: shows a value, never edits one. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui infolist-entry partial -- the read-only DISPLAY leaf of a detail page: the value variants the kit's infolist entries resolve to, one partial driven by a `type` switch. Mirrors io.lievit.kit.schema.infolist's read-only entries (the filament-infolists entry types carried over): ColorEntry / IconEntry / ImageEntry / CodeEntry. The READ counterpart of a form control: it shows a value, it never edits one. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure presentation; `value` is the already-projected display the controller resolved (ResolvedNode.Field.display / Entry.resolveDisplay). When empty, the caller passes the entry placeholder as `value` (Entry.placeholder), so the partial just shows the muted text; the kind switch only changes the chrome around a non-empty value. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the kit's Entry.kind() tag ("color" / "icon" / "image" / "code", default "text") is exactly the branch a renderer keys on (ResolvedNode.Field carries the kind), so ONE partial with a `type` switch is the server-first mirror of the sealed entry set rather than four near-identical partials. Each branch REUSES an existing primitive rather than re-implementing: the icon branch renders @template.lievit.icon, the image branch an avatar-style tile, the color branch a swatch + the @template.lievit.badge value, the code branch a 
.
  A "text" type falls through to the plain value (so callers can route every entry kind here).

  Per-type meaning (matching the kit):
    - color: ColorEntry -> a swatch chip of the CSS color `value` + the value text (copyable adds a
      title hint; the click-to-copy wire is the adopter's, no inline JS here).
    - icon:  IconEntry -> the Lucide icon named by `icon`, tinted by `color` (the boolean/enum-state
      mirror); `value` is the optional adjacent label.
    - image: ImageEntry -> an  tile sized by width/height, optionally circular (the disk-resolved
      URL is `value`, already a full URL by the time it reaches the template).
    - code:  CodeEntry -> a monospace 
 block of `value`; `language` tags it (data-language),
      lineNumbers toggles the gutter affordance class. Highlighting is the runtime's, not inline JS.

  TOKEN FIDELITY: swatch border + code surface read --lv-color-border / --lv-color-muted-bg; the
  icon tint maps a Color name to a --lv-color-* var; nothing is a raw hex.

  A11y: the color swatch is decorative (aria-hidden) with the value text carrying the meaning; the
  icon entry's icon is decorative unless `value` is absent, in which case pass `label` so AT reads
  it; image entries MUST carry alt (the partial requires `alt`); the code block is readable text.

  Params:
    String type     = "text"      color | icon | image | code | text (the kit Entry.kind()).
    String value    = ""          the projected display string (or the resolved URL for image).
    String icon     = null        (icon) the Lucide icon name the IconEntry resolved.
    String color    = null        (icon/color) a semantic colour token name (primary, success,
                                  warning, danger, info) or, for color type, the raw CSS color value.
    boolean copyable = false      (color/code) hint that a click-to-copy wire is attached.
    String alt      = null        (image) the required alt text for the image.
    Integer width   = null        (image) rendered width in px (intrinsic when null).
    Integer height  = null        (image) rendered height in px (intrinsic when null).
    boolean circular = false      (image) crop to a circle (an avatar-style tile).
    String language = null        (code) the highlighting language tag.
    boolean lineNumbers = false   (code) show the line-number gutter.
    String label    = null        (icon) accessible label when the icon stands alone.
    String cssClass = ""          extra utility classes on the entry root.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.infolistEntry(type = "color", value = "rgb(29 78 216)")
    @@template.lievit.infolistEntry(type = "icon", icon = "check", color = "success", value = "Verificato")
    @@template.lievit.infolistEntry(type = "image", value = "/avatars/1.jpg", alt = "Mario", circular = true, width = 40)
    @@template.lievit.infolistEntry(type = "code", value = "{\"ok\":true}", language = "json", lineNumbers = true)
--%>
@param String type = "text"
@param String value = ""
@param String icon = null
@param String color = null
@param boolean copyable = false
@param String alt = null
@param Integer width = null
@param Integer height = null
@param boolean circular = false
@param String language = null
@param boolean lineNumbers = false
@param String label = null
@param String cssClass = ""

!{var tintVar = switch (color == null ? "" : color) {
    case "primary" -> "var(--lv-color-primary)";
    case "secondary" -> "var(--lv-color-secondary)";
    case "success" -> "var(--lv-color-success)";
    case "warning" -> "var(--lv-color-warning)";
    case "danger", "destructive" -> "var(--lv-color-danger)";
    case "info" -> "var(--lv-color-info)";
    case "muted" -> "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";
    default -> "var(--lv-color-fg)";
  };}

@if(type.equals("color"))
  
    
    ${value}
  
@elseif(type.equals("icon"))
  
    @if(icon != null && !icon.isBlank())
      @template.lievit.icon(name = icon, label = (value == null || value.isBlank()) ? label : null)
    @endif
    @if(value != null && !value.isBlank())
      ${value}

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-group partial -- an input wrapped with leading and/or trailing addons (icon or text) inside one shared bordered box that focuses as a single control. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The control state lives in the native ; the group is layout + the focus-within border treatment, no client behaviour. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's InputGroup is a `role="group"` flex box with a border + shadow that owns the focus ring, while the inner is borderless/ringless (border-0, focus-visible:ring-0) so the GROUP shows focus. Addons are ordered start/end. We reproduce exactly that: the shared border + focus-within ring on the wrapper, a stripped inner input, and leading/trailing Content slots. shadcn wins styling; the addon-click-to- focus nicety is JS, omitted here (static tier) -- clicking the input itself focuses, and the whole box is visually one field, so no behaviour is lost for the common case. ADDON ALIGNMENT (shadcn's InputGroupAddon align): each addon carries a `data-align`. The default is inline (inline-start / inline-end, beside the control); `block-start` / `block-end` stack the addon ABOVE / BELOW the control instead (shadcn's block alignment, e.g. a toolbar row above a textarea). When a block addon is present the wrapper flips to a column + auto height via the `has-[>[data-align=block-*]]` selectors, exactly like shadcn. INVALID (shadcn parity with the input's aria-[invalid] cue): the `invalid` flag puts the group into the error state -- destructive border + a destructive ring on the WRAPPER (the group owns the ring, so the error shows on the box, matching @@template.lievit.input's aria-[invalid=true] treatment). items.name: input-otp
items.description: A segmented one-time-code input rendered server-side: N native single-character <input> slots + a hidden `name` mirror that submits the joined value in a plain POST form (zero wire round-trips mid-entry). The auto-advance / paste-fill / backspace-nav choreography is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer. Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-input-otp> Lit island. WAI one-time-password-field ARIA (role=group + per-slot Character N of M labels).

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-otp partial -- a segmented one-time-code input rendered SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012). N native single-character s + a hidden `name` mirror; the small auto-advance / paste / backspace-nav behaviour is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (input-otp.enhancer.ts), NOT inline script. Pairs with registry/jte/input-otp.enhancer.ts. TIER: PARTIAL (+ enhancer). The OTP value is NOT server state mid-entry: there is nothing for the server to decide between keystrokes, so round-tripping each keystroke over the wire would be pure chat for no gain. The slots are server-rendered native inputs; the value is form-submitted (the server binds it at POST via the hidden mirror under `name`), so it is server-bound at the moment that matters. The only client bit is the focus choreography (auto-advance, paste-fill, backspace step-back), which is a tiny typed-TS module the strict CSP accepts (no inline on* handlers). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI one-time-password-field (Radix), the model the dropped Lit island cited: a role="group" of real slots, each aria-label="Character N of M", the per-type inputmode, autocomplete="one-time-code" on the first slot so the platform SMS-autofill fills the whole code, and a visually-hidden mirror so `new FormData(form)` sees the joined value with zero JS. Native inputs beat a custom widget: the platform owns focus, IME, paste and autofill; the enhancer only nudges focus between slots. SEPARATOR + GROUPS: `groupSize` splits the slots into explicit visual groups (shadcn's InputOTPGroup), with an InputOTPSeparator (a Lucide dash) rendered between groups. `groupSize=3` on a length-6 code gives `***-***`. The separator is decorative (aria-hidden); the slots stay one continuous keyboard + value sequence (the enhancer indexes by data-otp-slot, not by group). onComplete: when set, the enhancer fires a `lievit:otp-complete` CustomEvent (detail = the joined value) on the root once every slot is filled, and -- if `onComplete="submit"` -- submits the enclosing
. Wire it to a wire action via the data-otp-complete attribute (the consuming wire template reads it), or listen for the event. Server-first: still no per-keystroke round-trip. A11y (WAI one-time-password-field): div[role=group][aria-label] wrapping N inputs each aria-label="Character N of M"; aria-invalid mirrored on the group + slots. Separators are aria-hidden decorations between visual groups. Params: name String submit field name; the joined value POSTs under it via the hidden mirror length int number of code slots (default 6) value String prefilled value (default empty); chars distributed across the slots type String "numeric" | "alphanumeric" | "alpha" (default "numeric"); drives inputmode + pattern mask boolean render slots as password fields (default false) disabled boolean disable every slot (default false) invalid boolean error ring + aria-invalid (default false) groupSize int split slots into groups of this size with a separator between (0 = no grouping) onComplete String "submit" submits the form when filled; any non-blank value enables the complete event label String accessible label for the slot group (default "One-time code") Usage: @template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", length = 6, type = "numeric") @template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", length = 6, groupSize = 3, onComplete = "submit") import the enhancer once from your entry module: import "./lievit/input-otp.enhancer"; --%> @param String name @param int length = 6 @param String value = "" @param String type = "numeric" @param boolean mask = false @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean invalid = false @param int groupSize = 0 @param String onComplete = null @param String label = "One-time code" !{var inputMode = type.equals("numeric") ? "numeric" : "text";} !{var pattern = type.equals("numeric") ? "[0-9]*" : (type.equals("alpha") ? "[a-zA-Z]*" : "[a-zA-Z0-9]*");} !{var slotType = mask ? "password" : "text";} !{var grouped = groupSize > 0;} !{var hasComplete = onComplete != null && !onComplete.isBlank();}
@for(int i = 0; i < length; i++) @if(grouped && i > 0 && i % groupSize == 0) @template.lievit.icon(name = "minus", size = "1rem") @endif !{var ch = i < value.length() ? String.valueOf(value.charAt(i)) : "";} !{var runStart = i == 0 || (grouped && i % groupSize == 0);} !{var runEnd = i == length - 1 || (grouped && (i + 1) % groupSize == 0);} !{var borderColor = invalid ? "var(--lv-color-danger)" : "var(--lv-color-border)";} !{var leftBorder = runStart ? ("1px solid " + borderColor) : "none";} !{var radius = runStart && runEnd ? "var(--lv-radius-sm)" : (runStart ? "var(--lv-radius-sm) 0 0 var(--lv-radius-sm)" : (runEnd ? "0 var(--lv-radius-sm) var(--lv-radius-sm) 0" : "0"));} @endfor

items.name: link
items.description: A standalone styled anchor: the Filament LinkComponent / the action LINK variant rendered as a real <a href>, with an optional leading or trailing Lucide icon and an optional trailing badge. Two variants drive the colour pair from the tokens: default (primary-coloured, underlines on hover, the shadcn link affordance) and muted (muted-foreground text darkening to full foreground on hover). A blank href degrades to an inert aria-disabled <span>; newTab opens in a new tab with rel=noopener noreferrer. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012): no client state, no island, zero JS, CSP-clean. The dedicated render for io.lievit.kit.component.LinkView, which lievit-ui previously folded into the button partial.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui link partial -- a standalone styled anchor: the Filament LinkComponent / the action LINK variant rendered as a real , with an optional leading/trailing Lucide icon and an optional trailing badge. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). A link carries no client state: it is a plain the browser navigates (middle-click + open-in-new-tab work), so the whole thing is server-rendered, zero JS, CSP-clean. lievit-ui folded links into the button partial until now; this is the dedicated render for io.lievit.kit.component.LinkView and for the action LINK variant, which need an anchor that LOOKS like a link, not a button. WHY a separate partial (link vs button): a button is an affordance for an ACTION; a link is an affordance for NAVIGATION. shadcn keeps them distinct (Button asChild= is still button-shaped; the "link" Button variant is the bridge). Filament's LinkComponent is its own primitive. Here a link is a text-coloured anchor (no button chrome), so it reads as a link inline in prose, in a toolbar, or as a row action -- exactly the LINK ActionVariant the kit dispatches. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): two variants drive the colour pair from the tokens so a retheme retints every link -- `default` (primary-coloured, underline-on-hover, the shadcn "link" button variant) and `muted` (muted-foreground text that goes full-foreground on hover, the toolbar / breadcrumb-tail affordance). An optional Lucide glyph composes the icon partial; its POSITION is `leading` (default) or `trailing` (iconPosition), matching Filament's icon-position knob. An optional trailing badge composes the badge partial (e.g. a "New" count next to the link text), so the link + its annotation render as one inline unit. Server-first polymorphism: a blank href renders an inert styled identically (the disabled / no-target case), mirroring how badge.jte / chip.jte degrade. TARGET: when `newTab` is set the anchor opens in a new tab with rel="noopener noreferrer" (the LinkView.newTab() / Filament openUrlInNewTab contract); a screen-reader hint is the caller's job via the label text, the partial only sets the safe rel. A11y: a real focusable with the platform anchor semantics + a focus-visible ring. The icon is decorative (aria-hidden, icon partial) -- the link TEXT names the destination, never the glyph alone. Colour is never the only signal: the text + the underline-on-hover carry the affordance. Params: String label = null the link text (used when no content slot is supplied). gg.jte.Content content optional rich children; takes precedence over label. String href = null the link target; blank => inert (no navigation). String variant = "default" "default" (primary, underlines on hover) | "muted". String icon = null optional Lucide icon name composed via the icon partial. String iconPosition = "leading" "leading" | "trailing" -- where the icon sits. String badge = null optional trailing badge text composed via the badge partial. String badgeVariant = "secondary" the trailing badge's variant. boolean newTab = false open in a new tab (target=_blank + rel=noopener noreferrer). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the link. "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)" : "var(--lv-color-primary)";} <%-- default mirrors the shadcn "link" affordance (underline on hover); muted darkens to full fg. --%> !{var hoverClass = "muted".equals(variant) ? "hover:text-[var(--lv-color-fg)]" : "hover:underline";} !{var linkClass = "lievit-link lievit-link--" + variant + " inline-flex items-center gap-[var(--lv-space-1)] text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)] font-[var(--lv-font-medium)] leading-none no-underline underline-offset-4 transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:shadow-[var(--lv-ring)] " + hoverClass + " " + cssClass;} !{var linkStyle = "font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);color:" + color + ";";} @if(isLink) @if(hasIcon && iconLeading)@template.lievit.icon(name = icon, size = "1rem")@endif @if(content != null)${content}@else${label}@endif @if(hasIcon && !iconLeading)@template.lievit.icon(name = icon, size = "1rem")@endif @if(hasBadge)@template.lievit.badge(variant = badgeVariant, label = badge)@endif @else @if(hasIcon && iconLeading)@template.lievit.icon(name = icon, size = "1rem")@endif @if(content != null)${content}@else${label}@endif @if(hasIcon && !iconLeading)@template.lievit.icon(name = icon, size = "1rem")@endif @if(hasBadge)@template.lievit.badge(variant = badgeVariant, label = badge)@endif @endif

items.name: modal
items.description: A centred modal dialog (shadcn Dialog), STATIC + trigger-driven, with ZERO JS. Built on the native HTML <dialog> element: the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (declarative open, no script, like popovertarget) and every close path (header x, backdrop, Escape) is a native <form method="dialog"> submit / the browser's modal default. The browser renders it in the top layer, traps focus, paints the ::backdrop, and exposes it as a modal dialog to AT, so the partial is CSP-clean with no enhancer. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby). Body + footer are OWNED Content slots, never <slot>. The presentation shell the kit's modal stack renders through: it carries the io.lievit.kit.component.ModalView affordances (closeButton, closeByClickingAway, closeByEscaping, width) and io.lievit.kit.ModalConfig chrome (heading/description, width xs..7xl/full/screen mirroring the kit Width/ModalWidth tokens, start/center alignment, submit/cancel footer slot). The trigger-driven static counterpart of the server-state-held wire dialog (registry/wire/dialog/). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens.

items.name: navigation-menu

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui navigation-menu partial -- a site navigation landmark with real links and optional rich dropdown panels, CSS-only (no JS, no client state). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 2): the Lit island is gone. TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL, not WIRE): a navigation menu is, by the ADR's driving rule, "pure display, no client state" once you accept the server-pure overlay. The links are real (the browser navigates, middle-click + open-in-new-tab work); which entry is current is a fact the SERVER already knows (the route being rendered), passed in as `activeKey` -- not client state. The only interactive bit, "reveal the panel", needs no server round-trip and no durable state, so it is pure CSS :hover / :focus-within (the same server-pure disclosure the hover-card partial uses). There is therefore nothing to hold server-side: no @Wire field, no action, no wire component. A WIRE conversion would round-trip the server just to flip a CSS-revealable panel -- strictly worse. Hence `registry:jte`, the simplest thing that works server-first. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): NOT a menu (no role="menu"); a navigation landmark, Radix NavigationMenu as the model the dropped island cited:
wrapping a
    of items. An item is either a bare link (navigation-menu/link) or a trigger that reveals a panel of real links (navigation-menu/menu). The intent-delay open / leave-close nuance of the island is dropped deliberately (a delay needs a timer = client state); a small padding bridge keeps the trigger->panel gap crossable. items.name: pagination
items.description: Page navigation: previous / next plus a windowed list of numbered page links with ellipsis gaps. Real <a href> per page, so it works JS-off; the caller passes the page math (current + total) and the partial derives the visible window (MUI-style) with shadcn's markup/token styling. A labelled <nav> landmark, aria-current on the active page, aria-disabled boundary controls.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: pagination (static JTE partial) Page navigation: previous / next plus a windowed list of numbered page links with ellipsis gaps. Mirrors shadcn's Pagination / PaginationContent / PaginationItem / PaginationLink / PaginationPrevious / PaginationNext / PaginationEllipsis. Tier rationale (planning two-tier model): pure presentation, no client state. The page links are REAL elements (server-rendered hrefs), so the control works with JavaScript disabled, which a Lit island could not claim. JTE partial it is. SHAPE DECISION (scientific rule). shadcn/MUI/Radix were compared for how the page list is produced. shadcn leaves the item list to the caller (hand-written s); MUI's DERIVES the windowed list (first, last, current +/- siblings, ellipsis for gaps) from count/page/siblingCount. MUI wins criterion (4) ergonomics + (5) robustness for the no-JS server case: the caller passes the page math (current + total), the partial computes the visible window once, so every page renders a correct, consistent control with no per-call boilerplate. We adopt MUI's derivation with shadcn's markup/token styling. HREF: page links are built from a printf-style `hrefPattern` (one %d placeholder for the page number), e.g. "/contatti?page=%d". This keeps the partial query-string-agnostic: the caller owns the URL shape, the partial only substitutes the number. A real per page. Accessibility (shadcn + APG): the root is
so AT lands on a named landmark; the active page carries aria-current="page" and is a non-link (you cannot navigate to where you already are); prev/next at the boundary become aria-disabled (still in the DOM for layout, not focusable); the ellipsis is aria-hidden with an sr-only "More pages". The chevron icons are decorative (Lucide partial, aria-hidden) with sr-only text labels. Params: int current the active page (1-based). int total the total number of pages (>= 1). String hrefPattern printf pattern for a page URL; one %d = the page number. int siblings pages shown either side of current before collapsing to "...". Default 1. String ariaLabel the
accessible name. Default "Pagination". String prevLabel sr-only text for the previous control. Default "Previous page". String nextLabel sr-only text for the next control. Default "Next page". String klass extra classes on the
. asChild / router-Link (server-first): shadcn's PaginationLink takes `asChild` so the page links can be a framework router-Link instead of a bare . Server-side the element stays a real (no-JS correctness), but the caller can OPT IN extra attributes on every navigable link via `linkAttrs` (a raw attribute string, output with $unsafe so it lands as markup, not escaped text), e.g. an htmx/Turbo/router hook that upgrades the anchor to an in-place navigation: `linkAttrs = "hx-boost=\"true\" hx-target=\"#list\""`. This is the server-first analogue of asChild: same accessible anchor, with the adopter's router wiring attached. Left blank, nothing is injected. Only the navigable links (prev / numbered / next) receive it; the disabled boundary spans and the ellipsis do not. Usage: @@template.lievit.pagination(current = 3, total = 10, hrefPattern = "/contatti?page=%d") @@template.lievit.pagination(current = page, total = totalPages, hrefPattern = "/listings?p=%d&q=" + query, siblings = 2) @@template.lievit.pagination(current = page, total = totalPages, hrefPattern = "/listings?p=%d", linkAttrs = "hx-boost=\"true\" hx-target=\"#list\"") --%> @param int current @param int total @param String hrefPattern @param int siblings = 1 @param String ariaLabel = "Pagination" @param String prevLabel = "Previous page" @param String nextLabel = "Next page" @param String linkAttrs = "" @param String klass = "" !{var page = Math.max(1, Math.min(current, total));} !{var hasPrev = page > 1;} !{var hasNext = page < total;} <%-- Windowed page list (MUI derivation): first + last always, current +/- siblings, gaps -> -1 sentinel (ellipsis). --%> !{var pages = new java.util.ArrayList();} !{var lastShown = 0;} @for(int p = 1; p <= total; p++) !{boolean show = p == 1 || p == total || (p >= page - siblings && p <= page + siblings);} @if(show) @if(lastShown != 0 && p - lastShown > 1) !{pages.add(-1);} @endif !{pages.add(p);} !{lastShown = p;} @endif @endfor
    <%-- Previous --%>
  • @if(hasPrev) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-left", size = "1rem") Prev @else @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-left", size = "1rem") Prev @endif
    • <%-- Numbered pages + ellipsis --%> @for(int p : pages)
  • @if(p == -1) @template.lievit.icon(name = "ellipsis", size = "1rem") More pages @elseif(p == page) ${p} @else ${p} @endif
    • @endfor <%-- Next --%>
  • @if(hasNext) Next @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-right", size = "1rem") @else Next @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-right", size = "1rem") @endif

Pass the active page (`current`), the total page count (`total`), and a printf-style `hrefPattern` (one %d = the page number, e.g. "/contatti?page=%d"); the partial derives the visible window (first + last always, current +/- `siblings`, ellipsis for gaps) and emits a real <a href> per page so it navigates with zero JS. Controls render at the h-9 (md) baseline, flush with a button / input of the matching size. Opt extra attributes onto every navigable link via `linkAttrs` (raw markup, e.g. an htmx/Turbo router hook). Requires the icon partial for the chevron + ellipsis glyphs. items.name: popover
items.description: A click-triggered floating panel anchored to a trigger, with ZERO JS. The load-bearing overlay seam (ADR-0012 blueprint R4): show/hide via the native HTML `popover` attribute + `popovertarget` (top-layer, light-dismiss, ARIA wired by the browser); position via CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name / position-anchor / position-area + flip-block fallback), no @floating-ui/dom. Panel content is OWNED, server-rendered markup (a Content slot), never a <slot>. Optional `title` + `description` render a PopoverHeader and make it a labelled dialog (role=dialog + aria-labelledby); an `align` axis (start/center/end via data-align + position-area inline span) and a decoupled PopoverAnchor (the popover/anchor sub-partial + `anchorId`) match shadcn. Replaces the <lv-popover> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean. For server-data-driven content + server-held open-state, see the popover-wire component.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui popover partial -- a click-triggered floating panel anchored to a trigger, with ZERO shipped JS. This is the LOAD-BEARING overlay seam (ADR-0012 blueprint R4): dropdown-menu, the calendar-filter popover and any anchored panel build on this exact mechanism. items.description: A searchable Combobox (Filament Select parity, roadmap L1) whose options are filtered SERVER-SIDE: the query binds l:model.debounce, the server re-filters the catalog and re-renders the listbox (the canonical wire/htmx typeahead, no client filtering). Supports single-select (the `selected` string, set via $set) AND multiple-select with removable chips (the `selectedValues` list, toggled via $set('toggleValue',...)), preload (eager full-catalog on mount for small sets) vs lazy debounced search (large sets), inline create-option (a no-match query arms a new value), and rich option labels (avatar/icon + subtext). Single mode: selection sets the `selected` field via $set('selected', '<value>'). Multiple mode (multiple=true): each pick / chip-remove toggles membership of its value in `selectedValues` via $set('toggleValue', '<value>'); the chosen options render as removable chips and submit as repeated hidden inputs (name=...). Preload (preload=true) shows the full catalog on mount for SMALL sets; leave it false and narrow `allOptions` server-side per keystroke for LARGE sets. allowCreate=true offers a 'Create "<query>"' affordance on a no-match query (value creation only; wire createOptionUsing server-side for a full form). items.name: slide-over
items.description: A modal panel that slides in from a viewport edge (right | left | top | bottom) -- the shadcn Sheet, STATIC + trigger-driven, with ZERO JS. The modal partial pinned to an edge: same native HTML <dialog> seam (trigger <button command="show-modal" commandfor>, close via native <form method="dialog"> + the browser's Escape default), so the browser owns top-layer rendering, focus-trap, ::backdrop and AT-modal exposure -- CSP-clean, no enhancer. The `side` (data-side) pins + sizes the panel to the chosen edge; the markup carries the shadcn sheet-* data-slot contract. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby). Header / body / footer are OWNED Content slots, never <slot>. The static presentation shell behind the kit's edge-anchored panels: io.lievit.kit.SlideOver (right-anchored detail) and any ModalConfig.asSlideOver(). The trigger-driven static counterpart of the server-state-held wire drawer / sheet (registry/wire/drawer/, registry/wire/sheet/), named slide-over to avoid the bare drawer/sheet wire item names. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens.

TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The RANGE (two-thumb) case is shadcn's Slider with two values: we render TWO native ranges, one per bound (POSTing `Min` / `Max`), overlaid on a shared track so both thumbs are real native sliders with real keyboard + a11y, no JS thumb math. VERTICAL uses the platform's native vertical range (writing-mode: vertical), not a rotate hack, so the keyboard arrows map to the visual axis. items.name: tabs
items.description: A tab bar + panels where the active tab is server state; only the active panel is shown. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the active id lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-tabs> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG tabs (tablist > tab[aria-selected][aria-controls] + tabpanel[aria-labelledby], roving tabindex). An HTMX content-swap recipe is the lighter alternative for independent panel fragments (see docs).
items.docs: Register TabsComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `tabIds` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `disabledIds`, as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.tabs(...)`. Each tab switches with `l:click="$set('active','<id>')"` (the active tab is a scalar, so the $set magic IS the select; no custom action needed); the @LievitRender hook falls back to the first enabled tab and refuses a disabled/unknown active id. The panel BODY per tab is OWNED markup inside the `data-tabs-panel` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). HTMX alternative: drop the wire and give each tab link an `hx-get` to a controller endpoint returning the panel partial, swapped into the panel container, when the panels are independent server fragments. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/tabs")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: toast
items.description: A transient overlay notification rendered server-side from a flash / notification: severity drives both colour and the ARIA live role (danger/warning are assertive role=alert, the rest are polite role=status) plus a leading Lucide icon. Variants cover the sonner surface: severity (info/success/warning/danger), a neutral `default`, a `loading` spinner, and `promise` (the resolved/rejected outcome rendered server-first). A single toast fixes itself to one of the six positions (top/bottom x left/center/right); render several inside the toast/viewport stack container (data-slot=toast-viewport). The auto-dismiss timer + dismiss button are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (no wire round-trip: a shown toast needs no server decision). Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-toast> Lit island.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui toast/viewport partial -- the fixed stack container that holds several toasts at one corner (sonner's Toaster region; data-slot="toast-viewport"). Server-rendered, zero JS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout (a fixed-corner flex column with a gap), no client state: the server already knows which toasts to show, so it renders them stacked. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): sonner renders ONE fixed region per corner that column- stacks its toasts. We reproduce that with a fixed flex column anchored to the chosen `position` (the six sonner corners) and a token gap between toasts. Bottom positions stack column-reverse so a new toast appears nearest the edge and pushes the older ones away from it, matching sonner. The toasts inside are rendered with standalone=false so they flow in this column instead of fixing themselves. A11y: a plain layout container; each child toast owns its own role=alert/status live region, so the viewport itself carries no role. Params: String position = "bottom-right" one of: top-left | top-center | top-right | bottom-left | bottom-center | bottom-right. gg.jte.Content content the @template.lievit.toast(standalone = false, ...) calls. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the viewport. Usage: @@template.lievit.toast.viewport(position = "top-right", content = @@` @@template.lievit.toast(standalone = false, variant = "success", content = @@`Saved.`) @@template.lievit.toast(standalone = false, variant = "info", content = @@`Heads up.`) `) --%> @param String position = "bottom-right" @param gg.jte.Content content @param String cssClass = "" !{var bottom = position.startsWith("bottom");} !{var posStyle = switch (position) { case "top-left" -> "top: var(--lv-space-4); left: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-start;"; case "top-center" -> "top: var(--lv-space-4); left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); align-items: center;"; case "top-right" -> "top: var(--lv-space-4); right: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-end;"; case "bottom-left" -> "bottom: var(--lv-space-4); left: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-start;"; case "bottom-center" -> "bottom: var(--lv-space-4); left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); align-items: center;"; default -> "bottom: var(--lv-space-4); right: var(--lv-space-4); align-items: flex-end;"; // bottom-right };}
${content}

items.docs: Copies the toast partial (+ its toast/viewport stack container) to your JTE root at lievit/toast.jte (reached as @template.lievit.toast(variant=..., content=@`...`)) and the CSP-clean enhancer to your alias root at lievit/toast.enhancer.ts. Render the partial from the server's flash / notification (e.g. @if(flash != null) @template.lievit.toast(variant = flash.level(), content = @`${flash.message()}`) @endif) so 'open' is simply 'rendered'. variant covers severity (info/success/warning/danger) plus default (neutral), loading (spinner) and promise (pass promiseState=success|danger to render the resolved outcome). A lone toast fixes itself to position (top/bottom x left/center/right); to stack several, wrap @template.lievit.toast(standalone = false, ...) calls in @template.lievit.toast.viewport(position = ...). Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllToasts() on load (and after any DOM swap): it starts the auto-dismiss timer (data-toast-duration) and wires the dismiss button. items.name: toggle-group
items.description: A set of toggle buttons with single- or multiple-selection; the selected value(s) are server state. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the selection lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-toggle-group> Lit island. Radix ToggleGroup a11y: single = radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple = group > button[aria-pressed].
items.docs: Register ToggleGroupComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `values` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `icons` (index-aligned, '' = none) and `disabledValues`, plus `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') and the whole-group `disabled`, as locked server config; the buttons render themselves. Each item toggles with `l:click="$set('toggleValue','<value>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the selected set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side; single mode keeps at most one and allows deselect-to-empty, Radix behaviour). Single mode renders role=radiogroup > radio[aria-checked], multiple renders role=group > button[aria-pressed]. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/toggle-group")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: wizard
items.description: A multi-step form (the Filament Wizard / Wizard\Step, the io.lievit.kit.schema.Wizard + io.lievit.kit.WizardAction view-models): a numbered step header where each step carries data-state complete/current/upcoming (a completed-check glyph when done, a number otherwise) with separators between steps, over the current step's content, and a footer of prev / cancel / next / submit actions. The cursor is server-owned; advancing is a real wire-action POST the adopter wires (next/previous/submit), never inline script. Reuses icon + separator. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui wizard partial -- a multi-step form (the Filament Wizard / Wizard\Step, the io.lievit.kit.schema.Wizard + io.lievit.kit.WizardAction view-models): a numbered STEP HEADER (each step a wizard/step item with a number / completed-check, label, and a separator between steps) over the current step's CONTENT, with a FOOTER of prev / cancel / next / submit actions. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012), ZERO shipped JS. TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL, not WIRE): the wizard's CURSOR (which step is current, which are complete) is server-owned data the Wizard driver already tracks (currentStep()); the partial renders the header + the current step's body + the footer for THAT cursor. Advancing (next / previous / submit) is a real wire-action POST the adopter wires on the footer buttons (an l:click or a form submit) -- the kit's WizardActionComponent drives next()/previous() and calls submit() on the last step. This partial is the pure VIEW of one cursor position; the host owns the state machine. (The kit's wizard-action.jte WIRE template composes THIS partial's chrome with l:click hooks; the registry ships the presentational primitive.) STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the Filament wizard header is a numbered stepper -- Radix/shadcn has no stepper, so this mirrors Filament's bespoke chrome with the shadcn data-state idiom. Each step carries data-state="complete" | "current" | "upcoming"; a complete step shows a check glyph instead of its number, a current step is filled, an upcoming step is muted. Steps are an
    (ordered, the natural numbering); a separator (the lievit-ui separator) sits between consecutive steps. The body is the `content` slot (the current step's fields); the footer is the `footer` slot (wizard/footer with the nav buttons). The currentStep index drives which step is `current` and which earlier steps are `complete`. A11y (WAI-ARIA): the header is an
      labelled "Progress"; the current step carries aria-current="step" so assistive tech announces the position; each step states its number + label as text (never the visual number alone); a complete step's check is aria-hidden (the "complete" state is conveyed by aria-label on the step). The body region is labelled by the current step's label. Params: String id = "lv-wizard" the wizard root id (labels the body region). Unique/page. int currentStep = 0 the 0-based current step index (drives complete/current). gg.jte.Content steps the step header items (wizard/step list, in order). gg.jte.Content content the current step's body (fields). gg.jte.Content footer = null the nav footer (wizard/footer: prev / cancel / next / submit). String label = "Progress" the step header's accessible name. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wizard root. Usage: @@template.lievit.wizard( currentStep = wizard.currentStep(), steps = @@` @@for(int i = 0; i < wizard.steps().size(); i++) @@template.lievit.wizard.step( index = i, label = wizard.steps().get(i).label(), state = i < wizard.currentStep() ? "complete" : (i == wizard.currentStep() ? "current" : "upcoming"), last = i == wizard.steps().size() - 1) @@endfor `, content = @@``, footer = @@`@@template.lievit.wizard.footer( showPrevious = wizard.currentStep() > 0, showSubmit = wizard.currentStep() == wizard.stepCount() - 1)`) --%> @param String id = "lv-wizard" @param int currentStep = 0 @param gg.jte.Content steps @param gg.jte.Content content @param gg.jte.Content footer = null @param String label = "Progress" @param String cssClass = "" !{var bodyLabelId = id + "-current-label";}
        ${steps}
      ${content}
      @if(footer != null)
      ${footer}
      @endif

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui wizard/step partial -- one item of the wizard step header (the Filament Wizard\Step in the progress strip, an io.lievit.kit.schema.Wizard.Step): a numbered marker (or a completed-check when done), the step label + optional description, and a separator trailing it (except after the last step). Server-first, no JS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The state (complete / current / upcoming) is server-owned data the Wizard driver computes from currentStep(); the server renders the marker keyed on it. When the step is already passed and the wizard allows jumping back (goToStep), the caller can pass a `href` so the step marker is a real the browser navigates -- otherwise an inert
  • . STRUCTURE (data-state, shadcn idiom): the step carries data-state="complete" | "current" | "upcoming". A COMPLETE step's marker shows a "check" glyph (the completed-check) on a primary fill; a CURRENT step's marker shows its 1-based number on a primary fill + a ring; an UPCOMING step's marker shows its number muted with a border. The label tints with the state (fg for current/complete, muted for upcoming). The trailing separator (the lievit-ui separator) is a flex-growing hairline between this step and the next; the LAST step (`last=true`) renders no separator. A11y (WAI-ARIA): the step is an
  • stating "Step N: (jump back). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the step. Usage: @@template.lievit.wizard.step(index = 0, label = "Details", state = "complete") @@template.lievit.wizard.step(index = 1, label = "Review", state = "current", last = true) --%> @param int index = 0 @param String label @param String description = "" @param String state = "upcoming" @param boolean last = false @param String href = "" @param String cssClass = "" !{var complete = "complete".equals(state);} !{var current = "current".equals(state);} !{var number = index + 1;} !{var stateWord = complete ? "completed" : (current ? "current" : "upcoming");} !{var markerBg = (complete || current) ? "var(--lv-color-primary)" : "transparent";} !{var markerFg = (complete || current) ? "var(--lv-color-primary-fg)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} !{var markerBorder = (complete || current) ? "transparent" : "var(--lv-color-border)";} !{var markerRing = current ? "box-shadow: var(--lv-ring);" : "";} !{var labelColor = (complete || current) ? "var(--lv-color-fg)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} !{var markerStyle = "width: 1.75rem; height: 1.75rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: " + markerBg + "; color: " + markerFg + "; border: 1px solid " + markerBorder + "; " + markerRing;} !{var isLink = !href.isEmpty();}
  • <%-- One tag (never a conditional / split: JTE's static checker pushes both opens). An with a null href drops the attribute and renders as inert text, so the non-link step is a plain anchor with no link semantics. --%> @if(complete)@template.lievit.icon(name = "check", size = "1rem")@else${number}@endif ${label} @if(!description.isEmpty()) ${description} @endif @if(!last) @template.lievit.separator() @endif

Server-first, no JS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Which buttons show is server-owned: PREVIOUS shows off the first step (showPrevious), SUBMIT replaces NEXT on the last step (showSubmit).
